title: UNIQUE dedup detection via error string contains("UNIQUE")
description:
feed.rs line 77: duplicate feed entries are detected by checking whether the error message string contains the substring "UNIQUE". This is fragile: it breaks if sqlx changes its error formatting, if a different constraint named UNIQUE-something fires, or if the message is localized.

The correct fix is to match on the sqlx error type. sqlx::Error::Database exposes a code() method; for SQLite, UNIQUE constraint violations have code "2067" (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE). Alternatively, match on sqlx::Error::Database and check .is_unique_violation() which is available on PostgreSQL and MySQL backends but not SQLite directly — for SQLite, check the constraint name via .message().

The safest drop-in: use sqlx::Error::Database(e) if e.message().contains("UNIQUE") — same brittleness, but at least it doesn't match on the outer anyhow chain. Better: INSERT OR IGNORE and check rows_affected() == 0.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
